steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: The GCF of $5x^2$ and $50xy^2$ is $5x$. Factor it out:$$5x^2-50xy^2=5x(x-10y^2)$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The factored form is $5x(x-10y^2)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '5x(x − 10y²)': Distributing gives 5x · x − 5x · 10y² = 5x² − 50xy², which matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — '5x(x − 50y²)': Distributing gives 5x² − 250xy², not 5x² − 50xy². ✗
  • Choice C — '5x²(10xy²)': This is a product, not a difference; it equals 50x³y², not the original expression. ✗
  • Choice D — '5x²(50xy²)': This equals 250x³y², not the original expression. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
